anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen-style practice-realization and inherited no-threshold practice cases.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in universality. The model needs no-boundary, no-threshold, practice-realization, inherited-practice, and analyst-frame-refused values before public use.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If practice and realization are inseparable, with no boundary in realization and no beginning in practice, then formal transfer marker may be an analyst imposition. In inherited religious practice, there may also be no initial relinquishment event for the model to locate.

claim_units: Formal transfer markers identify instructions, permissions, and prohibitions that change agency grammar at a stage or threshold.
Phenomenological dissolution is uneven across view, affective conceit, habit, narrative identity, and social credential.
Sharp formal boundaries can coexist with long lived afterwork, as in shinjin or recognition of rigpa.
Gradual paths can still focus safeguards around agency grammar changes.
A usable comparative rubric should code function, marker, stage interval, claimant layer, support holder, credited source, forbidden claimant, gradient width, stalling point, safeguard, and repair.
The most dangerous interval is not initial relinquishment but the aftermath of partial dissolution, where the practitioner may own the surrender itself.
Traditions should show specific safeguard clusters around these vulnerable intervals rather than only generic humility warnings.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Psychotherapy, addiction recovery, and leadership transition.
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: High-risk reappropriation will cluster after the formal change, when the person starts owning being recovered, humble, transformed, delegated, or healed. Programs with explicit post-boundary repair should outperform programs that treat the formal change as the main event.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A formal identity boundary, such as sobriety date, diagnosis acceptance, role transfer, or apology, can occur before psychological self-claiming releases across habit, affect, narrative, and social credential layers.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If relapse, status inflation, or identity rigidity is fully explained by habit strength, incentives, social support, clinical severity, and time since change, with no predictive gain from formal marker plus dissolution-gradient mismatch, the structure weakens.

exact_claim: Contemplative effort and gift should be coded as a two-track transfer profile: formal agency markers and phenomenological dissolution gradients. The highest risk comes after partial relinquishment, when remaining self-claiming reappropriates egolessness, surrender, recognition, or attainment.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Blind coders can identify agency markers from entry or stage passages, then predict warning clusters in separate manuals better than chance and better than generic humility themes.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Formal agency grammar changes predict where manuals place specific warnings and repairs.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-corpus manual coding across Theravada, Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen or Mahamudra, Soto Zen, Christian grace, and Sufi fana or baqa sources.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings distribute evenly, track only intensity or teacher authority, or cannot be predicted from formal markers.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioner interviews distinguish view-release, affective conceit, habit, narrative identity, and social credential layers, and each layer predicts different forms of owning the insight.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Partial dissolution produces layer-specific reappropriation patterns.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with practitioners who report partial awakening, shinjin, direct recognition, surrender, or nondual insight.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reports do not separate layers, or reappropriation is fully explained by ordinary pride, teacher feedback, status incentives, trauma, or community role.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Independent coders using each tradition's own vocabulary reach acceptable reliability on formal markers, forbidden claimant, support holder, and repair categories.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The rubric can be coded without importing Theravada categories into every tradition.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater trial with primary-text excerpts and practitioner review panels.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Reliability fails, or coders converge only after replacing indigenous terms with Buddhist fetter or conceit language.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Students given stage-specific repair language show fewer ownership distortions than students given only general humility, safety, or effort instructions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Repair instructions that explicitly address post-boundary reappropriation outperform generic anti-pride cautions.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Prospective training comparison in low-risk contemplative education settings.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Stage-specific repair adds no value after teacher relationship, screening, practice dose, group norms, and mental-health risk are controlled.

literature_search_queries: SN 22.89 Khemaka Sutta I am conceit identity view primary text
SN 45.8 Magga-vibhanga Sutta right effort desire persistence intent primary text
Tannisho Shinran entrusting mind Amida's working not self power primary text
Longchen Rabjam Trekcho instruction recognition stabilization conduct primary text
Steve Bein self-power other-power nondualism Japanese Buddhism article
Pargament religion problem solving process three styles coping self directing deferring collaborative
spiritual materialism ego converts meditation experience achievement Trungpa reappropriation source
Varieties of Contemplative Experience meditation related challenges influencing factors practice practitioner relationship context
Dogen Bendowa practice and realization are one primary text
Lindbeck doctrine as grammar rules discourse attitude action cultural linguistic model

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate generalizes this structure across traditions and pairs it with formal agency markers.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction. The core formal attainment versus residual dissolution distinction is already primary-textual.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the gradient claim. The text distinguishes abandoned self-view from residual I-am conceit and describes later uprooting through further practice.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns Shinran's internal grammar into a cross-tradition coding instrument.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for the formal-marker and reappropriation layers in Pure Land cases.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for sharp boundary, Other Power source, refusal of calculation, and warnings about claiming the practice as one's own.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate asks when such agency grammar changes or becomes forbidden, not merely whether effort is prescribed.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. It supplies a primary anchor, not the full comparative model.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for explicit agency grammar: the practitioner generates desire, endeavors, activates persistence, and exerts intent.

near_neighbors.difference: Dzogchen may not accept dissolution-gradient language, since the text emphasizes seeing the nature of mind rather than gradually dissolving a self-claiming layer.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and a strong distortion risk.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for recognition, critique of contrived effort, and post-recognition instructions that imply formal and ongoing dimensions.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate can handle Dogen only if it allows no-boundary and analyst-frame-refused values.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Reduces universality. It forces the rubric to become a local heuristic rather than a master structure.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close as an anomaly: practice and realization are inseparable, with no clean boundary in realization and no beginning in practice.

near_neighbors.difference: Pargament is not stage-sensitive contemplative-path analysis and does not model dissolution gradients.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the credit-distribution axis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for agency allocation among self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious styles.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds warning clusters, stage intervals, and gradient afterwork.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to large reduction, especially for Japanese Buddhist agency comparison.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for comparing self-power, other-power, and nondualism as agency structures in Japanese Buddhism.
near_neighbors.source: Steve Bein, Self Power, Other Power, and Non-dualism in Japanese Buddhism, https://philpapers.org/rec/BEISPO

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate makes reappropriation layer-specific and predicts where safeguards should cluster.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for the reappropriation thesis. Remaining novelty is operationalization.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the claim that ego appropriates spiritual practice, insight, surrender, and attainment.
near_neighbors.source: Chogyam Trungpa, Cutting Through Spiritual Materialism, summarized at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spiritual_materialism

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate predicts a narrower ownership and agency mechanism within that wider challenge ecology.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. VCE is a strong empirical baseline the model must outperform.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for qualitative coding of meditation-related challenges, interpretations, teacher responses, and contextual factors.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate uses grammar as a staged practice variable rather than a general theory of doctrine.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the grammar language.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for doctrine functioning as rules that regulate religious speech, action, and truth claims.
near_neighbors.source: George Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar model, discussed at https://www.scielo.org.za/scielo.php?pid=S2074-77052018000100007&script=sci_arttext

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate's main addition is the explicit split between formal marker and dissolution gradient, plus the warning-cluster prediction.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large internal reduction. Treat as consolidation and extension, not a standalone discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close for support locus, forbidden claimant, transfer timing, credit distribution, and claimability stance.
near_neighbors.source: Prior Lumenary records in prompt: Handoff Point, Credit Distribution, Capacity Ledger, Claimability and Support Rubric, Surplus Grain Test

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Reduce the rubric to a testable core before public use: function, formal marker, claimant layer, support holder, safeguard, repair, analyst-frame refusal.
Use split-source blind coding: code agency markers from entry or stage texts, then predict safeguards from separate manuals or practitioner interviews.
Add mandatory values for no-boundary, no-threshold, inherited-practice, practice-realization, Other Power refusal, recognition-event, continuous authorization, and undecidable.
Run inter-rater reliability before using cross-tradition claims.
Compare predictive value against Pargament agency styles, Lindbeck doctrine-as-grammar, Trungpa spiritual materialism, VCE contextual factors, upaya pedagogy, and prior Lumenary ledgers.
Ask practitioners for concrete failed-student cases and repair instructions, not agreement with the model.
Do not raise above extended unless the model predicts warning clusters or repairs better than prior Lumenary rubrics and established near-neighbor theories.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.61 to about 0.54. The two-track formulation is useful, but the primary-text close read shows that formal attainment versus residual self-claiming is already explicit in SN 22.89, Other Power boundary and anti-calculation repair are explicit in Shinran, and reappropriation is strongly anticipated by spiritual materialism. The remaining contribution is a comparative coding synthesis with testable warning-cluster predictions.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is standard fetter theory, Shinshu non-calculation, Dzogchen stabilization, spiritual materialism, upaya, or ordinary pastoral caution. They cannot name repairs beyond familiar warnings about pride, striving, doubt, role identity, or teacher dependence.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently identify formal thresholds and layer-specific reappropriation patterns, then report that the two-track rubric changes sequencing, student warnings, or repair language in ways not captured by existing models.
questions: Is the distinction between formal marker and lived release already standard in your lineage, or does it change how you diagnose students?
Where do your strongest warnings and repairs actually cluster: before entry, at a formal threshold, after partial realization, or throughout practice?
Can you name a concrete case where a student owned surrender, egolessness, recognition, humility, or grace in a predictable way?
Does this rubric identify a repair that would not already come from spiritual materialism, upaya, religious coping, or ordinary teacher experience?
Are there cases in your tradition where the entire question of transfer, ownership, or agency grammar is misleading?
relevant_practitioners: Theravada teachers familiar with fetter models and stream-entry discourse
Jodo Shinshu ministers familiar with shinjin and Other Power
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers familiar with recognition and stabilization
Soto Zen teachers familiar with practice-realization
Christian contemplative directors familiar with grace and surrender
Sufi teachers familiar with fana and baqa
Contemplative clinicians familiar with meditation-related difficulties

primary_pair: SN 22.89 Khemaka Sutta, Access to Insight, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn22/sn22.089.than.html
SN 45.8 Magga-vibhanga Sutta, Access to Insight, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The claimant-layer vocabulary is strongly shaped by Theravada categories and may distort Shinran, Dzogchen, Soto Zen, and traditions that reject agency accounting. The warning-cluster prediction also risks circular coding unless safeguards are coded from a separate corpus.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit found strong near-neighbors but did not fully close-read Steve Bein, Taitetsu Unno, Pargament, Lindbeck, Trungpa, VCE coding materials, Shinran's Kyogyoshinsho, Rennyo letters, or Tibetan manuals beyond the cited Longchen Rabjam text. It also did not check Pali, Japanese, or Tibetan terms directly.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 22.89 already separates a formal path achievement from a remaining gradient: the noble disciple has abandoned lower fetters and does not assume 'I am this,' yet residual 'I am' conceit, desire, and obsession remain until further contemplation uproots them. SN 45.8 gives a clear practitioner-agency grammar for right effort. Tannisho gives the opposite formal marker: entrusting, non-calculation, and Amida's working, while warning against claiming nembutsu as one's own practice. Cross-reading these texts supports the candidate's two-track architecture, but also shows that much of the architecture is already available in primary sources.

title: The Transfer Profile: Formal Boundaries and Dissolution Gradients in Contemplative Practice
unlike_statement: Unlike Khemaka, Shinran, Dzogchen instructions, Dogen, Pargament, Bein, Trungpa, VCE, and prior Lumenary ledgers, this idea says the comparative unit is a two-track transfer profile and predicts that safeguards cluster where formal agency grammar changes while partial dissolution leaves layer-specific reappropriation risks.
